seoTitle: How to Send a Test Email in Brevo Before Launch
metaDescription: Check your Brevo campaign for layout glitches and broken links before sending, using the built-in test email tool and a saved internal test list.
intro: Blasting an unvetted campaign draft straight to your full subscriber list risks embarrassing layout glitches and broken links. Brevo's test email tool lets you preview exactly how a campaign renders before committing to the real send. This guide covers setting up a test list and running a preview send.

steps.heading: Open your campaign in the designer
steps.body: Access the drag-and-drop designer viewport for the campaign you want to test before it goes out to your full list.

steps.heading: Build an internal test list
steps.body: Create and save a group test list containing your core staff or team member email handles so you have a consistent set of test recipients.

steps.heading: Send the test email
steps.body: Use the test send option in the campaign dashboard to push an instant preview to your saved test list.

steps.heading: Check rendering across devices
steps.body: Review how the email renders on both desktop and mobile views to catch any layout issues before the real campaign goes out.

steps.heading: Confirm links and merge tags
steps.body: Click through any links and check that personalization tags display correctly rather than showing broken bracket placeholders.

faq.question: Does sending a test email count against my sending limit?
faq.answer: Test sends are generally treated separately from bulk campaign sends, though it's worth checking your specific plan's terms.

faq.question: Can I send a test to multiple people at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can build and save an internal test list containing multiple recipients rather than testing to a single address each time.

faq.question: Why do my merge tags show up empty in the test email?
faq.answer: This usually happens because test recipients aren't part of your real contact list and don't have the custom attribute data the merge tags pull from.

faq.question: Should I test on mobile as well as desktop?
faq.answer: Yes, checking both is important since email layouts often render differently across mobile and desktop mail clients.
